name: Onion Rye
recipeIngredient: 1 egg beaten with 1 tablespoon water -- for glaze
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast and sugar in the warm water.
2. In a food processor or mixer, combine 1 cup of bread flour, salt, half a cup of rye flour, milk, egg, butter, Dijon mustard, onion, caraway seeds, and sesame seeds.
3. Add the yeast mixture.
4. Beat in the rest of the rye flour and any remaining flour necessary to make a soft but firm dough. The dough will still be slightly sticky.
5. Place the dough in an oiled plastic bag or oiled bowl. Seal or cover and let rise until doubled in size, or refrigerate overnight.
6. Punch down the dough and shape into an oval.
7. Place on a greased baking sheet.
8. Let double.
9. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
10. Brush the loaf with the egg glaze.
11. Bake until the bottom sounds hollow when tapped, about 45 to 50 minutes.
12. Remove from baking sheet and cool on a rack.
Yield: 1 loaf
